message:
chore: linter refinements — CLS skeleton for pricing viz, unmount-safe Canvas error, React slider test fix

- globals.css: explicit min-height on pricing-comparison skeleton to prevent
  CLS when dynamic viz mounts near FAQ after hash navigation
- header-icon-3d.tsx: guard Canvas onError with isMountedRef to prevent
  setState-after-unmount race
- tests: use expectVisualizationLoaded helper for pricing-calc test;
  use Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor for React-compatible slider fill
- beads: sync hgjp.41 close state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","title":"Legal / UPL review pass on article + primer before publish","description":"# Legal / Unauthorized Practice of Law (UPL) review gate

The article markets a tool that produces estate-planning drafts. State bars take an active interest in unauthorized practice of law. Publishing language that can be construed as "this tool replaces a lawyer" is legally risky for Jeffrey personally AND for jsm.md as a business.

## Review checklist (applies to article + primer + install copy)
- [ ] No claim that the skill "writes your will" — only "produces drafts for attorney review."
- [ ] No claim that the skill "is legal advice" — explicit disclaimer in .18 + primer + FAQ.
- [ ] No attorney-client relationship implied or created.
- [ ] No state-specific guarantees ("Texas readers can sign this will" — WRONG; "Texas-specific execution formalities must be verified with Texas counsel" — RIGHT).
- [ ] No before/after comparisons that imply a specific outcome for a specific reader.
- [ ] Disclaimer surfaces early enough that a reader cannot miss it (hero, install section, FAQ, TL;DR).
- [ ] Primer markdown has disclaimer on page 1.
- [ ] Testimonial claims (if any) are framed as user-reported, not guaranteed.
- [ ] Comparison block (.38) does not disparage competitors — stick to factual differences.

## Who reviews
- Jeffrey, as the author and subscription operator.
- Optionally: one pass by a licensed attorney friendly to the project (if Jeffrey has one available). Even an hour of review from a practicing estate-planning attorney is cheap insurance.

## Outcome
A comment on this bead confirming review was completed, who reviewed, and whether any language changes were required. If an attorney reviewed, record their initials.

## Hard gate
This bead BLOCKS `.31` (final commit/push) and `.34` (content QA). Article does not publish until this clears.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every disclaimer checkbox above confirmed.
- Comment recording the review is added to this bead before close.
- Article + primer language updates (if any) shipped as part of this bead or as discovered-from follow-ups.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:09:07.960752193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:05:39.258537273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:09:07.960752193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.932519801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:53.835916291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.37","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.123720543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:12:54.026210015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","title":"Scaffold article component (components/wills-estate-article.tsx)","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-article.tsx` as a "use client" component modeled exactly on `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`.

## Scope of this bead (scaffold only — sections land in follow-up beads)
- Imports: React, Next `Image`, Next fonts (Crimson_Pro, JetBrains_Mono, Bricolage_Grotesque), lucide-react icons (tentative: FileDown, ScrollText, ShieldCheck, HeartPulse, HandCoins, Landmark, Sparkles, BookOpen), `MathTooltip`, `getJargon` from `@/lib/wills-estate-jargon`, `getScrollMetrics` from `@/lib/utils`, and illustration asset.
- Dynamic imports for the five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz) from `./wills-estate-visualizations`, each `{ ssr: false }` because they use browser APIs / framer-motion.
- Font variables set up the same way as slack article (`--font-crimson`, etc).
- Wrapper components defined locally (these can be copy-pasted from slack article with the `sm-` → `wep-` class-name rename if any styles diverge; otherwise keep `sm-` so we share the stylesheet): `J` (jargon tooltip), `EC` (editorial container, 800px centered), `Divider`, `Code`, `Mono`, `SectionHeader`, `Sub`, `RefTable`.
- Primer download button constant: `PRIMER_HREF = "/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md"`, `PRIMER_FILENAME = "WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md"`.
- Main exported component `WillsEstateArticle` with:
  - `scrollProgress` state + scroll listener (copy pattern from slack).
  - IntersectionObserver-driven section reveal (copy pattern from slack, reuses `sm-fade-section` / `sm-visible` classes in globals.css).
  - Outer `div` with ref, role="main", className combines `sm-scope sm-body` + font variable classes, same background `#020204` and text `#f8fafc`.
  - Scroll progress bar rendered at top.
  - Empty section placeholders (stubs with TODO comments) for each section bead to fill in: hero, download-cta, intro, catalog, install, who-this-is-for, core-insight, sample-session, produces, workflow, anti-patterns, verification, tldr.

## Why a single scaffold bead
Every content-section bead will modify this file. Scaffolding it once means downstream section beads can land in parallel without file-creation conflicts. Per AGENTS.md "Backwards Compatibility & File Sprawl" rule: one article component, not ten.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists, `bun tsc --noEmit` is clean once the route + jargon beads land too.
- Has placeholder stubs for every section.
- Uses the existing `sm-*` CSS classes until we confirm we need a `wep-*` namespace (decided in the styles bead).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T01:00:40.849606492Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T01:00:40.846743209Z","close_reason":"Article scaffold created at components/wills-estate-article.tsx (604 lines, tsc clean). Includes: 5 dynamic viz imports, J/EC/Divider/Code/Mono/RefTable/SectionHeader/Sub helpers, MarkdownDownloadButton with PRIMER_HREF, scroll progress bar, IntersectionObserver section reveals, 12 anchor IDs (all frozen IDs + intro), 16 TODO stubs for downstream beads, closing pattern section with TL;DR sign-off.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:55.142041930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","title":"Record + embed install-flow screencast in article install section","description":"# Embedded screencast/GIF of full install flow in the article

## Why
Non-technical readers will read "paste a prompt into Claude Desktop, the agent installs the skill" and picture something alien. A 30-60 second silent video showing the full flow on screen is an order of magnitude more convincing. Existing software marketing pages universally use this pattern.

## Content
Silent screencast (no audio needed; the article is read in a browser tab).

Sequence to capture:
1. On jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill, cursor hovers the "Generate install prompt" button; button pressed; textarea appears with the prompt visibly populated + countdown "Expires in 10:00".
2. "Copy" button pressed; subtle confirmation.
3. Switch window to Claude Desktop (or Codex Desktop — can produce two variants and A/B which feels clearer).
4. Paste into the chat input; Enter.
5. Agent's tool-use confirmation dialog appears; "Allow" clicked.
6. Agent's streaming response: "Downloading... Extracting... Verifying..."
7. On-screen success: agent says "The skill is installed. Reload skills to see it in the picker."
8. Cmd-Shift-P → "Reload skills" → skill appears.
9. User types "/wills" → skill invokes → opening prompt appears.

Approximate 45-second runtime. No voiceover. Subtitles/captions overlay at key moments ("Generate code", "Paste into Claude", "Agent installs", "Done — skill available").

## Production
- Record on mac-mini-max in 1440p, downscale for web.
- Edit with the existing video tooling (see the obs-youtube-music skill if Jeffrey uses it).
- Export formats:
  - MP4 (H.264): mainline, broad browser support.
  - WebM (VP9): smaller; Chromium-family browsers will prefer.
  - GIF fallback: for email preview + contexts that won't play video.
- Poster image: a single high-quality frame showing the install card mid-flow.

## Placement in the article
- Inline inside the install section (hgjp.11), immediately after the description of Card 1 "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)".
- Use Next's <video> component with:
  - poster={...}
  - preload="metadata" (saves bytes; user clicks play to load the full thing)
  - controls
  - muted + playsInline (for autoplay-on-scroll behaviour if desired, which I'd keep off by default — autoplay is annoying)
  - Captions via <track kind="captions">; author SRT or WebVTT alongside.
- Lazy-load: the <video> element only fully loads when scrolled into viewport.

## Accessibility
- Captions required (WCAG SC 1.2.2).
- An expandable text description of the same flow immediately below the video (serves screen readers + anyone who skips video).
- Reduced-motion: keep autoplay disabled; respect prefers-reduced-motion by showing the poster as a static image.

## Storage
- Asset file at assets/install-screencast.mp4 etc.
- Served directly from Next's public/ (or via a CDN reference if too large for public/).
- Target file sizes: MP4 < 5 MB, WebM < 3 MB. Higher-quality variants available as optional downloads for anyone who wants them.

## Tests
- Playwright: assert the video element is present, poster loads, captions track is wired.
- Manual QA: play on Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Mobile Safari. Play on reduced-motion. Play with autoplay disabled in browser settings.

## Acceptance criteria
- Screencast recorded, edited, captioned.
- Embedded in hgjp.11 install section.
- Static poster + expandable text description both present.
- No regressions on Lighthouse (bd-3t7zd.29 — uh, hgjp.29) — the video should not delay LCP since it's below-the-fold.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56.988622402Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:06.571263626Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.880431851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:02.767148100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.40","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Video production + hosting strategy.
(1) Hosting: do not host a multi-MB video on the same origin as the article — hurts LCP budget and Vercel bandwidth bills. Use Cloudflare Stream (generous free tier) or Mux (best DX). Absolute worst case, use Vercel Blob and accept the cost. The article's <video> src points at the CDN URL.
(2) Captions REQUIRED per WCAG SC 1.2.2. Author WebVTT (.vtt) alongside the video. Never ship without captions.
(3) Transcript below video: full text of the captions as a screen-reader-friendly expandable panel — also serves as fallback for users who skip video entirely.
(4) Poster image: a single well-composed frame (install card with prompt visible + countdown) — shows immediately; avoids the 'grey void while video loads' on slow connections.
(5) Do NOT autoplay. Respect user intent. Autoplay is annoying and violates the site's design ethos. The poster + play icon is enough affordance.
(6) Reduced-motion: if prefers-reduced-motion is set, show only the poster image + a link to 'Watch how the install flow works (45s video).' User must click through; no autoplay, no hover-preview.
(7) Dark/light mode: record under the same scheme as the rest of the article (dark background). Since it's a screen recording of jsm.md, the app's dark/light theme is what shows in the video. Pick the mode that looks best (likely dark) and stick with it.
(8) File sizes: MP4 (H.264) < 5 MB for compatibility; WebM (VP9) < 3 MB for bytesavers. Both served via <source>.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:56Z"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","title":"Interactive pricing calculator (attorney vs LegalZoom vs jsm)","description":"# Interactive pricing calculator inside the article

## Why
The static comparison block (hgjp.38) puts the dollar amounts on the page, but readers don't internalise them unless they see their own numbers. An interactive calculator makes it visceral. 

The goal is not precision — the goal is "holy crap, that's a lot of money I could save."

## Placement
Inline inside the article, near or inside hgjp.38 (comparison section). NOT a separate section with its own anchor — this is a visual embedded widget, small and scannable.

## Inputs (minimal — two rows)
- Net worth slider: $100k → $100M (log scale); defaults to $1M.
- Family situation (multi-select chips, 0+ selected):
  - Blended family
  - Minor children or dependents
  - Business or partnership interest
  - Vacation home / multi-state property
  - Vulnerable heir (addiction, disability, capacity concerns)
  - Non-citizen spouse
  - Crypto self-custody
  - Concentrated stock / pre-IPO

## Outputs (three lines, typeset large)
- Attorney consultation estimate: derived from net worth + complexity factor
  - Formula (deliberately rough): base $3000 + $0.00005 per $ of net worth above $1M + $2000 per complexity chip.
  - Capped at $40k for the "HNW with every complication" reader.
- LegalZoom / Trust & Will estimate: static $199-499 regardless of inputs (they don't scale with complexity; that's precisely the point).
- Jeffreys Skills.md: $20/month (cancel anytime).

Below the three figures, a small footnote: "Attorney estimates are indicative only; actual quotes vary widely by state and firm. Our estimate uses public billing-rate data (cite one or two sources) for transparency."

## UX details
- Updates live as the user drags or toggles.
- Savings tag: "Projected savings vs attorney consult: $N,NNN." — animated on every input change.
- Smooth transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Keyboard-accessible: slider supports arrow keys, chips are toggle buttons.
- A11y: aria-live on the output row so screen readers get updates.

## Implementation
Pure client-side React component in components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx or a sibling file. No server round-trips; every calculation is local.

## Honest framing
Add a one-line disclaimer: "This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote. Actual attorneys quote based on actual facts. Your real skill subscription is $20/month — the attorney number is so you understand the order of magnitude."

## Tests
- Slider moves across its full range; every position produces a plausible number (no NaN, no negative).
- All 8 chips toggle independently; outputs reflect the change.
- Analytics: pricing_calc_changed (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) fires on each input; gives us insight into what readers actually have.

## Acceptance criteria
- Widget renders in the comparison area.
- All interactions smooth + accessible.
- Disclaimer explicit.
- Analytics events wired.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:02:09.432970939Z","close_reason":"Implemented PricingComparisonViz: log-scale net worth slider, 8 complexity chips, attorney/LegalZoom/jsm cost comparison, animated savings, a11y (aria-live, keyboard, reduced motion), GA4 analytics, DoNotTrack respect, disclaimer. Wired via viz-error-boundary dynamic import into the comparison section. E2E test added.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.312610349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.206312451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Pricing calc honesty + edge handling.
(1) Attorney rate data source citations: the formula's base + per-$ + per-chip coefficients must be derivable from public data (ABA billing-rate surveys, state bar reports). Add a sources paragraph below the calc: 'Rates derived from <ABA 2024 billing report>, <Martindale survey>, etc.' Without this the numbers feel pulled from thin air.
(2) Soft clamps: slider max is M; formula may produce silly numbers like k attorney for a k net worth (below the realistic -3k floor for a basic estate plan). Clamp outputs to a reasonable range (min k, max k for attorney; -499 for LegalZoom).
(3) Disclaimer proximity: the honest-framing sentence ('This calculator is a rough guide, not a legal fee quote') must be visually adjacent to the output numbers, not buried. Same font weight as the numbers themselves.
(4) No PII captured by the calc. Outputs derived client-side; analytics event records bucketed inputs (net_worth_bucket, num_chips) not raw values.
(5) Respect prefers-reduced-motion: slider live updates are animations; dampen them.
(6) Accessibility: slider is a native <input type='range'> with explicit min/max/step + aria-label; chips are toggle <button> with aria-pressed; outputs in aria-live='polite' region.","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}

- Disclaimer explicit.
- Analytics events wired.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:10:34.118468685Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:10:34.115598158Z","close_reason":"Implemented and committed — PricingComparisonViz with slider, chips, three-way comparison, a11y, analytics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:32.001688634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.312610349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.206312451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.41","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Pricing calc honesty + edge handling.

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","title":"Draft/preview mode for the article (pre-launch reviewer sharing)","description":"# Preview mode / share-token for pre-launch article

## Why
The legal / UPL review bead (hgjp.39), the content QA bead (hgjp.34), and the non-technical friend walkthrough (hgjp.34) all benefit from sharing a URL to the article BEFORE it ships. Also: Jeffrey might want one or two trusted reviewers (an attorney, a friend who read the tax article) to eyeball it before it hits Twitter.

Today, if we merge to main, Vercel publishes to production. There's no middle state.

## The design

### Option A: Vercel preview deployments (simplest)
Every PR or non-main branch auto-gets a preview URL from Vercel. Share that URL with reviewers. This already exists; the "new" thing is just using it deliberately.

Pro: zero code. Con: Vercel preview URLs are publicly accessible (no auth); anyone with the URL sees the article. For a launch article this is probably fine.

### Option B: Preview-token gate on the production site
Add a small middleware that checks ?preview=<token> query param OR a cookie. If the current article is within a "pre-publish" date window AND no valid token is present, serve a 404 (or a "launching soon" landing).

- Reserved tokens stored in a new small config in the repo (or in a database if jsm.md is doing this for all articles — but the personal-site is a static-first Next site, so a small table or env-backed list is fine).
- Tokens are opaque strings; minted on demand via a personal script.
- Token activates the article; without it the route is hidden.

### Recommended: Option A + small polish
Use Vercel preview deployments as the share mechanism. Add a lightweight `draft: true` frontmatter/flag to the article metadata; when draft, Next explicitly removes the article from /sitemap.xml, from the writing index (hgjp.5 registration), and from the command palette. When draft=false (publish day), the article surfaces everywhere.

This is the cleanest separation: draft state is a simple boolean in the article's metadata, reviewed in the content QA pass, flipped atomically at launch.

## Implementation

### Article metadata
```tsx
export const metadata = {
  title: "...",
  description: "...",
  alternates: { canonical: "/writing/wills-and-estate-planning" },
  robots: { index: false, follow: false }, // while draft
};

const DRAFT = true; // flip to false on launch day
```

### lib/content.ts
Skip draft articles when building the writing index:
```ts
if (!post.draft) writingList.push(post);
```

### sitemap.ts
Same: omit draft articles.

### command-palette.tsx
Same: omit draft articles from search index.

### Publishing
- Throughout development, draft=true; shareable via vercel-<branch>-<sha>.vercel.app URLs.
- Launch commit flips draft=false + robots: index, follow.

## Tests
- Playwright: in draft mode, /writing does not list the article; /sitemap.xml does not include it; Cmd-K "wills" returns no result. Yet direct visit to /writing/wills-and-estate-planning still renders (for authorized reviewers sharing the URL).
- Launch mode (draft=false): article appears in /writing, /sitemap.xml, command palette.

## Acceptance criteria
- draft flag implemented and wired to all three surfaces.
- Publishing is one commit: flip the flag.
- Search engines do not index draft articles (meta robots noindex).
- Tests cover both states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.884555586Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:34:58.881681355Z","close_reason":"Made draft-mode publication surfaces consistent for MDX-backed writing entries: writing index, sitemap, RSS, API search, and route metadata now honor draft visibility while direct preview URLs stay renderable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:20:59.171182267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW round-3 2026-04-19: Draft-mode safety belt.
(1) meta robots: while draft=true, the page emits <meta name='robots' content='noindex, nofollow'>. Verified via Playwright test that fetches the draft URL and asserts the header.
(2) Accidental index monitoring: add a simple once-daily cron that fetches the draft article URL, inspects the response for the noindex meta tag; if missing, page the operator. Catches the case where someone flips draft=false earlier than intended.
(3) The draft flag must be checked in THREE places per the bead description: sitemap.ts, lib/content.ts writing list, command-palette search index. Test all three — easy to miss one.
(4) Preview URL shareability: Vercel preview URLs are guessable enough that a reviewer who shares a screenshot can't inadvertently leak the URL via metadata. If that's a concern, add HTTP Basic auth to Vercel preview (Vercel Pro feature) or shrink the review window.
(5) Launch procedure: the commit that flips draft=false should ONLY flip that flag. Not bundled with other changes. Diff is one line; trivially reviewable.
(6) Post-launch: after the draft flag flips and the article is live, remove the robots-noindex cron check (it's no longer needed).","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:23:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation:

## 1. `lib/content.ts`
Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include:
- slug: `wills-and-estate-planning`
- title: final article title
- description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card
- date: publication date
- tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]`
- href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
- hero image reference

## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search)
Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered.

## Why this bead is separate
These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article shows up on `/writing`.
- `Cmd+K` → "wills" / "estate" / "planning" surfaces the article.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:45.372547993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:45.360674112Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.910594517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T23:23:03.532754813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset.

## Purpose
This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as:

1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill).
2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output.
3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead).

## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/)
- `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer`
- TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality.
- The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters.
- Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each).
- The 9 intake phases as a numbered list.
- The 9-step workflow as a numbered list.
- The top 15 anti-patterns.
- The verification-first discipline (live-law checks).
- Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/).
- A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents.
- Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill"

## Format
- Plain Markdown.
- ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide).
- Semantic headings so an agent can index it.

## Acceptance criteria
- File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`.
- Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename.
- Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","notes":"test","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.785078713Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:49:43.781833618Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"test comment","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:38Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Primer size target corrected from 8-12 KB to 30-60 KB (hard cap 200 KB). The primer is the **travelling alternative to the full skill** — if a non-subscriber pastes it into a fresh Claude/Codex/ChatGPT session, they should be able to conduct a meaningful 9-phase intake, name all 12 axioms + 14 operators, and produce a minimum-viable deliverable set (asset-inventory, beneficiary-map, red-flag-triage, letter-of-instruction, attorney-engagement-brief). This is the single highest-leverage marketing asset on the whole article. Trim state-specific appendices (volatile anyway) before trimming axioms/operators.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:56:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-spoh","title":"Document pre-existing performance optimizations","description":"# Pre-existing Performance Optimizations Audit

## Part of Feature
Performance Optimization Initiative

## Objective
Document optimizations that were already in place, providing institutional knowledge for future development.

## Findings

### 1. Dynamic Imports (Code Splitting)

#### client-shell.tsx (Lines 17-22)
```tsx
const CommandPalette = dynamic(() => import("@/components/command-palette"), {
  ssr: false,
});
const KeyboardShortcutsModal = dynamic(() => import("@/components/keyboard-shortcuts-modal"), {
  ssr: false,
});
```
**Status:** ✅ Already optimized
**Impact:** ~22KB deferred until modal interaction

#### hero.tsx (Three.js Scene)
```tsx
const ThreeScene = dynamic(() => import("@/components/three-scene"), {
  ssr: false,
  loading: () => <div className="..." />,
});
```
**Status:** ✅ Already optimized
**Impact:** ~854KB deferred until visible

### 2. React.memo Usage

#### flywheel-visualization.tsx
```tsx
const FocalPane = memo(function FocalPane({ ... }) {
  // ... expensive component
});
```
**Status:** ✅ Properly memoized
**Reason:** Complex component with many children, renders on hover state change

### 3. useMemo/useCallback Patterns

#### TldrToolGrid (Search State)
```tsx
const fuse = useMemo(() => new Fuse(tools, { ... }), [tools]);
const filteredTools = useMemo(() => { ... }, [fuse, searchQuery, tools]);
const { coreTools, supportingTools } = useMemo(() => { ... }, [filteredTools]);
```
**Status:** ✅ Properly memoized
**Impact:** Prevents Fuse.js re-initialization on every render

#### command-palette.tsx
```tsx
const staticCommands = useMemo<Command[]>(() => { ... }, [router, onClose]);
const filteredCommands = useMemo(() => { ... }, [staticCommands, query, fuse, router, onClose]);
const groupedCommands = useMemo(() => { ... }, [filteredCommands]);
const handleKeyDown = useCallback((e: React.KeyboardEvent) => { ... }, [filteredCommands, selectedIndex, onClose]);
```
**Status:** ✅ Comprehensive memoization
**Impact:** Smooth keyboard navigation and search

### 4. useReducedMotion Pattern

Found in 15+ components:
```tsx
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
const reducedMotion = prefersReducedMotion ?? false;

// Animation conditional
initial={reducedMotion ? {} : { opacity: 0, y: 20 }}
transition={{ duration: reducedMotion ? 0 : 0.4 }}
```
**Status:** ✅ Consistent accessibility pattern
**Impact:** Respects user preference, reduces motion sickness risk

### 5. CSS Performance Patterns

#### motion-safe/motion-reduce in Tailwind
```tsx
className="... motion-safe:group-hover:translate-x-full motion-reduce:transition-none"
```
**Status:** ✅ CSS-level motion reduction
**Impact:** Animations disabled at CSS level, no JS evaluation needed

#### Backdrop Blur Optimization
```css
backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
-webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px);
```
**Status:** ✅ Hardware accelerated
**Note:** Modern browsers GPU-accelerate backdrop-filter

### 6. Intersection Observer Usage

#### stats-grid.tsx
```tsx
const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
  ([entry]) => {
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      setIsVisible(true);
      observer.disconnect();
    }
  },
  { threshold: 0.3 }
);
```
**Status:** ✅ Lazy animation trigger
**Impact:** Stats only animate when visible, disconnect prevents memory leaks

### 7. Image Optimization

Next.js Image component used throughout:
```tsx
<Image
  src="/jeff_emanuel_headshot.webp"
  alt="Jeffrey Emanuel"
  width={640}
  height={640}
  priority  // Above the fold
  placeholder="blur"
  blurDataURL={HEADSHOT_BLUR_DATA_URL}
/>
```
**Status:** ✅ Optimized loading
**Impact:** WebP format, blur placeholder, priority loading

## Recommendations for Future Work

1. **Maintain patterns**: Continue using these established patterns
2. **Don't over-optimize**: Current level is appropriate for site scale
3. **Monitor bundle size**: Use `ANALYZE=true npm run build` periodically
4. **Test on low-end devices**: Ensure animations don't degrade UX

## Conclusion
The codebase already had strong performance foundations. The additional optimizations in this initiative complement and extend these patterns without disrupting them.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:52:43.786148653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T22:54:26.846141221Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T22:54:26.846141221Z","close_reason":"Documented all pre-existing optimizations: dynamic imports, React.memo usage, useMemo/useCallback patterns, useReducedMotion, CSS motion patterns, IntersectionObserver, and Next.js Image. Knowledge preserved for future development.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-t2g9","title":"E2E tests for performance optimization verification","description":"# E2E Tests for Performance Optimization Verification

## Part of Feature
Performance Optimization Test Suite (jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-p59k)

## File to Create
tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts

## Objective
Verify that the performance optimizations have not broken any user-facing functionality through comprehensive E2E tests.

## Test Cases Required

```typescript
import { test, expect } from "@playwright/test";

/**
 * E2E Tests for Performance Optimizations
 *
 * These tests verify that the useMemo, React.memo, and seededRandom
 * optimizations have not broken any functionality.
 *
 * Run with: bunx playwright test tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts
 * Debug with: PWDEBUG=1 bunx playwright test tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts
 */

test.describe("Performance Optimization - Stats Grid", () => {
  test("stats display correctly on homepage", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing stats grid functionality after memoization");

    await page.goto("/");
    console.log("[E2E] Navigated to homepage");

    // Wait for stats to be visible
    console.log("[E2E] Waiting for stats section");
    const statsSection = page.locator("dl");
    await expect(statsSection).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] Stats section visible");

    // Check that stats display numbers
    console.log("[E2E] Verifying stat values are displayed");
    const statValues = page.locator("dd");
    const statCount = await statValues.count();
    console.log(`[E2E] Found ${statCount} stat values`);
    expect(statCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3);

    // Verify at least one stat contains a number
    const firstStat = statValues.first();
    const text = await firstStat.textContent();
    console.log(`[E2E] First stat value: "${text}"`);
    expect(text).toMatch(/\d/);
    console.log("[E2E] Stats grid test passed");
  });

  test("stats animate when scrolled into view", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing animated stats (after useMemo optimization)");

    await page.goto("/");

    // Scroll to stats section
    console.log("[E2E] Scrolling stats into view");
    const statsSection = page.locator("dl");
    await statsSection.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded();

    // Wait a moment for animation
    await page.waitForTimeout(500);

    // Stats should show final values
    console.log("[E2E] Checking stat values after animation");
    const statValues = page.locator("dd");
    const firstStat = await statValues.first().textContent();
    console.log(`[E2E] Stat value after animation: "${firstStat}"`);

    // Should contain a formatted number (not 0 or empty)
    expect(firstStat).not.toBe("0");
    expect(firstStat).not.toBe("");
    console.log("[E2E] Stats animation test passed");
  });

  test("stats work with reduced motion preference", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing stats with reduced motion");

    await page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: "reduce" });
    await page.goto("/");

    // Stats should display immediately without animation
    console.log("[E2E] Checking stats display immediately");
    const statsSection = page.locator("dl");
    await expect(statsSection).toBeVisible();

    const statValues = page.locator("dd");
    const firstStat = await statValues.first().textContent();
    console.log(`[E2E] Stat value with reduced motion: "${firstStat}"`);

    // Should have the final value immediately
    expect(firstStat).not.toBe("0");
    console.log("[E2E] Reduced motion stats test passed");
  });
});

test.describe("Performance Optimization - Tool Grid Search", () => {
  test("search functionality works after React.memo optimization", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing tool grid search after React.memo optimization");

    await page.goto("/tldr");
    console.log("[E2E] Navigated to /tldr");

    // Find search input
    const searchInput = page.getByPlaceholder(/search tools/i);
    await expect(searchInput).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] Search input visible");

    // Type search query
    console.log("[E2E] Typing search query session");
    await searchInput.fill("session");

    // Wait for filter
    await page.waitForTimeout(300);

    // Should show filtered results
    console.log("[E2E] Checking filtered results");
    await expect(page.getByText(/showing/i)).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] Search functionality works correctly");
  });

  test("section headers render correctly after React.memo", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing section headers after React.memo");

    await page.goto("/tldr");

    // Check both section headers exist
    console.log("[E2E] Checking Core Flywheel Tools header");
    await expect(
      page.getByRole("heading", { name: /core flywheel tools/i })
    ).toBeVisible();

    console.log("[E2E] Checking Supporting Tools header");
    await expect(
      page.getByRole("heading", { name: /supporting tools/i })
    ).toBeVisible();

    console.log("[E2E] Section headers test passed");
  });

  test("empty state displays correctly", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing empty state after React.memo optimization");

    await page.goto("/tldr");

    const searchInput = page.getByPlaceholder(/search tools/i);
    await searchInput.fill("xyznonexistent123");

    await page.waitForTimeout(300);

    console.log("[E2E] Checking empty state message");
    const emptyState = page.locator("text=/no tools match/i");
    await expect(emptyState.first()).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] Empty state test passed");
  });
});

test.describe("Performance Optimization - 3D Scene", () => {
  test("hero 3D scene loads without errors", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing 3D scene after seededRandom fix");

    // Listen for console errors
    const errors: string[] = [];
    page.on("console", (msg) => {
      if (msg.type() === "error") {
        errors.push(msg.text());
        console.log(`[E2E] Console error: ${msg.text()}`);
      }
    });

    await page.goto("/");
    console.log("[E2E] Navigated to homepage");

    // Wait for scene to load
    await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
    console.log("[E2E] Waited for 3D scene to initialize");

    // Check no errors related to Three.js or canvas
    const threeErrors = errors.filter(
      (e) => e.includes("THREE") || e.includes("WebGL") || e.includes("canvas")
    );
    console.log(`[E2E] Found ${threeErrors.length} Three.js related errors`);
    expect(threeErrors).toHaveLength(0);
    console.log("[E2E] 3D scene test passed - no errors");
  });

  test("3D scene respects reduced motion", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing 3D scene with reduced motion");

    await page.emulateMedia({ reducedMotion: "reduce" });
    await page.goto("/");

    // Page should load without crashing
    console.log("[E2E] Checking page loads with reduced motion");
    await expect(page.locator("body")).toBeVisible();

    // Hero content should be visible
    const heroHeading = page.getByRole("heading").first();
    await expect(heroHeading).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] 3D scene reduced motion test passed");
  });
});

test.describe("Performance Optimization - No Visual Regressions", () => {
  test("homepage renders completely", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing homepage complete render");

    await page.goto("/");
    await page.waitForLoadState("networkidle");

    // Check key sections exist
    console.log("[E2E] Verifying key sections");
    await expect(page.locator("header")).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.locator("main")).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.locator("footer")).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] Homepage renders completely");
  });

  test("tldr page renders completely", async ({ page }) => {
    console.log("[E2E] Testing /tldr complete render");

    await page.goto("/tldr");
    await page.waitForLoadState("networkidle");

    // Check key sections
    console.log("[E2E] Verifying tldr page sections");
    await expect(
      page.getByRole("heading", { name: /agentic coding flywheel/i })
    ).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.getByPlaceholder(/search/i)).toBeVisible();
    console.log("[E2E] /tldr page renders completely");
  });
});
```

## Logging Requirements
Every test step must include:
1. console.log describing the action
2. console.log showing result
3. Clear pass/fail indication

## Running Tests
```bash
# Run all performance optimization tests
bunx playwright test tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts

# Run with debug UI
PWDEBUG=1 bunx playwright test tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts

# Run specific test
bunx playwright test tests/e2e/performance-optimization.spec.ts -g "stats display"
```

## Verification Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass on desktop viewport
- [ ] All tests pass on mobile viewport (375x667)
- [ ] No console errors during tests
- [ ] Tests complete in under 30 seconds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T23:11:23.367256550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T01:34:39.405899816Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T01:34:39.405899816Z","close_reason":"Implemented 13 comprehensive E2E tests verifying performance optimizations work correctly: stats grid (3 tests), tool grid search (3 tests), 3D scene (2 tests), visual regression (3 tests), mobile viewport (2 tests).  {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-tii","title":"Feature: Live GitHub star counts (auto-updating)","description":"# Live GitHub Star Counts

## Overview
Replace hardcoded star counts with live data fetched from GitHub API, ensuring the TL;DR page always shows accurate, up-to-date information.

## Current State
- Star counts hardcoded in `lib/content.ts` (tldrFlywheelTools array)
- Example: `stars: 1400` for MCP Agent Mail
- Becomes stale over time
- Hero stats "3,600+" is manually updated

## Proposed Solution

### Option A: Build-Time Fetching (Recommended)
- Fetch star counts during `bun run build`
- Store in generated JSON file
- Revalidate periodically via ISR

### Option B: Client-Side Fetching
- Fetch on page load
- Show skeleton while loading
- Cache in localStorage with TTL

### Option C: API Route + SWR
- Server route fetches and caches
- Client uses SWR for automatic revalidation
- Best balance of freshness and performance

## Technical Implementation (Option A)

### Build-Time Script
```typescript
// scripts/update-star-counts.ts
async function fetchStarCounts() {
  const repos = [
    { id: 'ntm', repo: 'Dicklesworthstone/ntm' },
    { id: 'mail', repo: 'Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail' },
    // ... all repos
  ];
  
  const counts: Record<string, number> = {};
  
  for (const { id, repo } of repos) {
    const res = await fetch(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}`, {
      headers: { 'Authorization': `token ${process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN}` }
    });
    const data = await res.json();
    counts[id] = data.stargazers_count;
  }
  
  // Write to file
  await writeFile(
    'lib/generated/star-counts.json',
    JSON.stringify({ counts, updatedAt: new Date().toISOString() })
  );
}
```

### Package.json Script
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build:with-stats": "bun run update-stats && bun run build",
    "update-stats": "bun run scripts/update-star-counts.ts"
  }
}
```

### Content Integration
```typescript
// lib/content.ts
import starCounts from './generated/star-counts.json';

export const tldrFlywheelTools = baseTools.map(tool => ({
  ...tool,
  stars: starCounts.counts[tool.id] ?? tool.stars // Fallback to hardcoded
}));
```

## Implementation (Option C - API Route)

### API Route
```typescript
// app/api/star-counts/route.ts
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';

export const revalidate = 3600; // Cache for 1 hour

export async function GET() {
  const repos = [...]; // Tool repo mappings
  const counts = await Promise.all(
    repos.map(async ({ id, repo }) => {
      const res = await fetch(`https://api.github.com/repos/${repo}`);
      const { stargazers_count } = await res.json();
      return { id, stars: stargazers_count };
    })
  );
  
  return NextResponse.json({
    counts: Object.fromEntries(counts.map(c => [c.id, c.stars])),
    total: counts.reduce((sum, c) => sum + c.stars, 0),
  });
}
```

### Client Usage with SWR
```tsx
function useStarCounts() {
  const { data, error } = useSWR('/api/star-counts', fetcher, {
    revalidateOnFocus: false,
    dedupingInterval: 60000,
  });
  return { counts: data?.counts, total: data?.total, isLoading: !data && !error };
}
```

## Hero Stats Integration
```tsx
// Calculate total from actual counts
const totalStars = useMemo(() => {
  return tools.reduce((sum, tool) => sum + (tool.stars || 0), 0);
}, [tools]);

// Display: "3,648" instead of hardcoded "3,600+"
<AnimatedStat value={formatStarCount(totalStars)} label="GitHub Stars" />
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- `scripts/update-star-counts.ts` (new) - Build-time script
- `lib/generated/star-counts.json` (generated) - Cached counts
- `app/api/star-counts/route.ts` (optional) - API route
- `lib/content.ts` - Integrate live counts
- `components/tldr-hero.tsx` - Dynamic total

## Rate Limiting Considerations
- GitHub API: 60 requests/hour unauthenticated
- With token: 5000 requests/hour
- 13 repos = 13 requests, well within limits
- Consider batch with GraphQL for single request

## GraphQL Alternative (Single Request)
```graphql
query {
  ntm: repository(owner: "Dicklesworthstone", name: "ntm") {
    stargazerCount
  }
  mail: repository(owner: "Dicklesworthstone", name: "mcp_agent_mail") {
    stargazerCount
  }
  # ... all repos
}
```

## Caching Strategy
- Build-time: Updated on each deploy
- API route: 1-hour cache with ISR
- Client: SWR with 1-minute dedup

## Fallback Behavior
- If API fails, show last known good value
- If no cached value, show hardcoded fallback
- Never show 0 or blank

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Star counts fetched from GitHub API
- [ ] Hero total calculated from actual counts
- [ ] Caching prevents excessive API calls
- [ ] Graceful fallback on API failure
- [ ] Works in build and runtime
- [ ] Respects GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Updated at least daily in production","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:56:00.611043201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:18:10.375647239Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:18:10.375558124Z","close_reason":"done - implemented build-time fetch in scripts/fetch-tldr-stars.ts with JSON cache and content.ts merge","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-tii","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-d20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-tii","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r0i","type":"child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
